# Support

> [!NOTE]
> BookWorm is a demonstration project, not production software. Support is provided on a best-effort basis by volunteer maintainers.

## Before You Ask

Please take a few minutes to check existing resources before opening a new issue or discussion:

1. **Search existing issues**: <https://github.com/foxminchan/BookWorm/issues>
2. **Search existing discussions**: <https://github.com/foxminchan/BookWorm/discussions>
3. **Read the documentation**: <https://foxminchan.github.io/BookWorm/>
4. **Review the contribution guide**: [CONTRIBUTING.md](./CONTRIBUTING.md)
5. **Check the project README**: [README.md](../README.md)

## How to Get Help

### Documentation

| Resource                                                    | Description                                         |
| ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| [Project README](../README.md)                              | Overview, goals, and quickstart                     |
| [Architecture docs](https://foxminchan.github.io/BookWorm/) | Detailed architecture documentation (Docusaurus)    |
| [EventCatalog](../docs/eventcatalog/)                       | Event schemas, service contracts, and async flows   |
| [CONTRIBUTING.md](./CONTRIBUTING.md)                        | Development workflow, coding standards, and testing |
| [AGENTS.md](../AGENTS.md)                                   | AI-assisted development workflow                    |

### GitHub Discussions

Use [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/foxminchan/BookWorm/discussions) for:

- **Questions** — "How does X work?" or "Why is Y designed this way?"
- **Ideas** — Suggestions for new features or improvements before filing an issue
- **Show and tell** — Share something you built using BookWorm as a reference

Please use the appropriate discussion category and provide as much context as possible.

### Bug Reports

Found something broken? [Open a bug report](https://github.com/foxminchan/BookWorm/issues/new?template=bug-report.yml) and include:

- A clear description of the problem
- Steps to reproduce the issue
- Expected vs. actual behavior
- Your environment: OS, .NET SDK version, Node.js version, Docker version
- Relevant logs or error messages

### Feature Requests

Have an idea? [Submit a feature request](https://github.com/foxminchan/BookWorm/issues/new?template=feature-request.yml) and describe:

- The problem you're trying to solve
- Your proposed solution
- Any alternatives you've considered

### Security Vulnerabilities

**Do not report security vulnerabilities as public issues.**

Please follow the process described in [SECURITY.md](./SECURITY.md) to report vulnerabilities privately.

## Response Times

BookWorm is maintained by volunteers. Please be patient:

| Request type     | Target response time |
| ---------------- | -------------------- |
| Bug reports      | 48–72 hours          |
| Feature requests | 1–2 weeks            |
| Pull requests    | Within 1 week        |
| Security reports | 48 hours             |

Response times are not guaranteed — complex issues or periods of low activity may take longer.

## Community Guidelines

When seeking help, please:

- ✅ Be respectful, patient, and constructive
- ✅ Provide complete, reproducible examples
- ✅ Search for existing answers before posting
- ✅ Follow up when your issue is resolved (it helps others!)
- ❌ Do not demand immediate responses
- ❌ Do not open duplicate issues or discussions
- ❌ Do not use issues as a general discussion forum

All interactions are subject to the [Code of Conduct](./CODE-OF-CONDUCT.md).

## Out-of-Scope Support

The following are outside the scope of this project's support:

- General .NET, ASP.NET Core, or Next.js questions unrelated to BookWorm
- Production deployments (BookWorm is a demo and is not production-ready)
- Customizations or forks that diverge significantly from this repository
- Third-party services (Keycloak, Kafka, PostgreSQL, etc.) — refer to their own support channels
